tasks.subject: extension-manifest-settings [P1] Update barrel exports in extension-api index.ts
tasks.description:
Update the barrel export file at `packages/extension-api/src/index.ts` to export the new types and schemas added in task 1.1.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add new type exports from manifest-schema.js

The current type export block from `manifest-schema.js` (lines 9-14) exports:
```typescript
export type {
  ExtensionManifest,
  SecretDeclaration,
  DataProxyConfig,
  ServerCapabilities,
} from './manifest-schema.js';
```

Add `SettingOption` and `SettingDeclaration` to this block:
```typescript
export type {
  ExtensionManifest,
  SecretDeclaration,
  SettingOption,
  SettingDeclaration,
  DataProxyConfig,
  ServerCapabilities,
} from './manifest-schema.js';
```

### 2. Add schema exports

The current schema export (line 8) only exports `ExtensionManifestSchema`:
```typescript
export { ExtensionManifestSchema } from './manifest-schema.js';
```

Add the new schemas:
```typescript
export {
  ExtensionManifestSchema,
  SettingOptionSchema,
  SettingDeclarationSchema,
} from './manifest-schema.js';
```

### 3. Add SettingsStore type export from server-extension-api.js

The current export block from `server-extension-api.js` (lines 22-26) exports:
```typescript
export type {
  SecretStore,
  DataProviderContext,
  ServerExtensionRegister,
} from './server-extension-api.js';
```

Add `SettingsStore` (which will be created in task 2.1):
```typescript
export type {
  SecretStore,
  SettingsStore,
  DataProviderContext,
  ServerExtensionRegister,
} from './server-extension-api.js';
```

Note: The `SettingsStore` interface doesn't exist yet (created in task 2.1), so this export will initially cause a TypeScript error. There are two approaches:

- **Option A (recommended):** Add only the manifest-schema exports now, and add the `SettingsStore` export in task 2.1 when the interface is created. This keeps each task independently shippable.
- **Option B:** Add all exports now and accept the transient type error until task 2.1 lands.

Go with Option A: add only `SettingOption`, `SettingDeclaration`, `SettingOptionSchema`, and `SettingDeclarationSchema` in this task.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `import type { SettingOption, SettingDeclaration } from '@dorkos/extension-api'` works
- `import { SettingOptionSchema, SettingDeclarationSchema } from '@dorkos/extension-api'` works
- Existing imports from `@dorkos/extension-api` are unaffected
- `pnpm typecheck` passes for the `packages/extension-api` package
tasks.activeForm: Updating extension-api barrel exports with new setting types and schemas

tasks.subject: extension-manifest-settings [P2] Update extension-server-api-factory.ts to include settings in DataProviderContext
tasks.description:
Update `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-server-api-factory.ts` to instantiate `ExtensionSettingsStore` and include it in the `DataProviderContext`.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add import

Add the `ExtensionSettingsStore` import alongside the existing `ExtensionSecretStore` import (line 10):

```typescript
import { ExtensionSecretStore } from '@dorkos/shared/extension-secrets';
import { ExtensionSettingsStore } from '@dorkos/shared/extension-settings';
```

### 2. Instantiate the settings store

In the `createDataProviderContext` function, after the secrets store instantiation (line 45), add:

```typescript
const secrets = new ExtensionSecretStore(extensionId, dorkHome);
const settings = new ExtensionSettingsStore(dorkHome, extensionId);
```

Note the parameter order difference: `ExtensionSecretStore` takes `(extensionId, dorkHome)` while `ExtensionSettingsStore` takes `(dorkHome, extensionId)`. The settings store follows the dork-home rule pattern (dorkHome as first required param).

### 3. Add settings to the context object

Update the `ctx` object construction (lines 82-89) to include `settings`:

```typescript
const ctx: DataProviderContext = {
  secrets,
  settings,
  storage,
  schedule,
  emit,
  extensionId,
  extensionDir,
};
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `createDataProviderContext` returns a `ctx` with a `settings` field
- The `settings` field is an `ExtensionSettingsStore` instance
- `pnpm typecheck` passes for `apps/server`
- Existing `secrets`, `storage`, `schedule`, and `emit` continue to work unchanged

tasks.subject: extension-manifest-settings [P4] Update Linear Issues example manifest with placeholder
tasks.description:
Update the Linear Issues example extension manifest at `examples/extensions/linear-issues/extension.json` to include `placeholder` on the `linear_api_key` secret.

## Implementation Steps

Update `examples/extensions/linear-issues/extension.json`. The current manifest has:

```json
"secrets": [
  {
    "key": "linear_api_key",
    "label": "Linear API Key",
    "description": "Settings -> API -> Personal API keys at linear.app",
    "required": true
  }
]
```

Add the `placeholder` field:

```json
"secrets": [
  {
    "key": "linear_api_key",
    "label": "Linear API Key",
    "description": "Settings -> API -> Personal API keys at linear.app",
    "placeholder": "lin_api_xxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "required": true
  }
]
```

This provides a realistic format hint showing the Linear API key prefix pattern.

Also check if there's a copy at `apps/server/.temp/.dork/extensions/linear-issues/extension.json` — that file is a dev runtime copy and may be auto-generated. Do NOT modify it; it will be regenerated from the example on next reload.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `examples/extensions/linear-issues/extension.json` includes `placeholder: "lin_api_xxxxxxxxxxxx"` on the `linear_api_key` secret
- The manifest still parses correctly with `ExtensionManifestSchema.safeParse()`
- No changes to any other files in the example extension
tasks.activeForm: Adding placeholder hint to Linear Issues example extension manifest

tasks.subject: extension-manifest-settings [P4] Update extension authoring guide with settings documentation
tasks.description:
Update `contributing/extension-authoring.md` to document the new settings declaration system, placeholder/group fields on secrets, and the `ctx.settings` API.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Read the current file

Read `contributing/extension-authoring.md` to understand its current structure and find the right insertion points.

### 2. Update the Secrets section

Find the existing section that documents `serverCapabilities.secrets` and add documentation for the new `placeholder` and `group` fields:

```markdown
#### Secret Fields

| Field         | Type    | Required | Description                                      |
| ------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `key`         | string  | Yes      | Secret key (lowercase snake_case)                |
| `label`       | string  | Yes      | Human-readable label for the settings UI         |
| `description` | string  | No       | Help text shown below the input                  |
| `placeholder` | string  | No       | Custom placeholder hint (e.g., `lin_api_xxxx`)   |
| `required`    | boolean | No       | Whether the extension cannot function without it |
| `group`       | string  | No       | Group name for collapsible section organization  |
```

### 3. Add a new "Settings Declaration" section

Add a section documenting the `settings` array. Place it after the Secrets section:

```markdown
### Settings Declaration

Declare non-secret configuration fields in `serverCapabilities.settings`. The host auto-generates a settings form — no UI code required.

#### Setting Types

| Type      | Input Component | Save Behavior          | Extra Properties       |
| --------- | --------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------- |
| `text`    | Text input      | Explicit save button   | `placeholder`          |
| `number`  | Number input    | Explicit save button   | `placeholder`, `min`, `max` |
| `boolean` | Toggle switch   | Immediate on toggle    | —                      |
| `select`  | Dropdown        | Immediate on selection | `options` (required)   |

#### Setting Fields

| Field         | Type                    | Required | Description                        |
| ------------- | ----------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `type`        | string                  | Yes      | One of: text, number, boolean, select |
| `key`         | string                  | Yes      | Setting key (lowercase snake_case) |
| `label`       | string                  | Yes      | Human-readable label               |
| `description` | string                  | No       | Help text shown below the input    |
| `placeholder` | string                  | No       | Placeholder text for text/number   |
| `default`     | string/number/boolean   | No       | Default value                      |
| `required`    | boolean                 | No       | Whether the setting is required    |
| `group`       | string                  | No       | Group name for collapsible section |
| `options`     | `{label, value}[]`      | select   | Options for select fields          |
| `min`         | number                  | No       | Minimum value (number fields)      |
| `max`         | number                  | No       | Maximum value (number fields)      |

#### Example Manifest with Settings

```json
{
  "id": "github-sync",
  "name": "GitHub Sync",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "serverCapabilities": {
    "secrets": [
      {
        "key": "github_token",
        "label": "GitHub Token",
        "placeholder": "ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx",
        "group": "GitHub",
        "required": true
      },
      {
        "key": "linear_api_key",
        "label": "Linear API Key",
        "placeholder": "lin_api_xxxx",
        "group": "Linear"
      }
    ],
    "settings": [
      {
        "type": "number",
        "key": "refresh_interval",
        "label": "Refresh Interval",
        "description": "How often to poll for updates (seconds)",
        "default": 60,
        "min": 10,
        "max": 3600
      },
      {
        "type": "boolean",
        "key": "show_archived",
        "label": "Show Archived Issues",
        "default": false,
        "group": "GitHub"
      },
      {
        "type": "select",
        "key": "sort_order",
        "label": "Sort Order",
        "options": [
          { "label": "Newest first", "value": "desc" },
          { "label": "Oldest first", "value": "asc" }
        ],
        "default": "desc"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### Grouping

When secrets and settings share the same `group` value, they render together in a collapsible section. Ungrouped items appear at the top. Within each group, secrets render before settings.
```

### 4. Update the DataProviderContext Quick Reference

Find the section that documents `DataProviderContext` and add the `settings` field:

```markdown
| `ctx.settings.get(key)`     | Read a non-secret configuration value. Returns `null` if not set. |
| `ctx.settings.set(key, val)` | Store a configuration value (string, number, or boolean).        |
| `ctx.settings.delete(key)`  | Remove a stored value (reverts to manifest default).              |
| `ctx.settings.getAll()`     | Read all settings as a key-value record.                          |
```

### 5. Add a best practice note

Add a note clarifying when to use secrets vs settings:

```markdown
> **Secrets vs Settings**: Use `secrets` for credentials, API keys, and tokens — these are encrypted at rest. Use `settings` for everything else: refresh intervals, display toggles, filter selections, label prefixes. Settings are stored as plaintext JSON.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Extension authoring guide documents all four setting types (text, number, boolean, select)
- Guide includes a complete example manifest with both secrets and settings
- Guide documents `placeholder` and `group` fields on secrets
- Guide documents `ctx.settings` API (get, set, delete, getAll)
- Guide includes secrets vs settings best practice note
- Guide includes grouping explanation
tasks.activeForm: Updating extension authoring guide with settings documentation
